description: Sending an actuator command that exceeds position, velocity, or torque limits because no final-stage clamp guards the hardware.
body: Failure mode: an upstream controller or planner produces a command beyond the actuator's safe envelope and it reaches the hardware interface without a final limit check, driving the joint past its range or applying damaging torque. Detection signals: telemetry shows commands outside the rated envelope; sustained actuator saturation; an injected out-of-range command reaches the hardware unmodified. Do not trust upstream stages to respect limits. Safer fallback: define per-actuator position/velocity/acceleration/torque limits, clamp every command at the last stage before the hardware interface, treat sustained saturation as a fault, and test that an injected out-of-range command is clamped and flagged.
